# neurovol { #neuroim.neurovol }

```python
neurovol(data, space, label='', indices=None)
```

Create a NeuroVol object.

Factory function that creates the appropriate NeuroVol subclass
based on the input data.

## Parameters {.doc-section .doc-section-parameters}

| Name    | Type                                           | Description                                        | Default    |
|---------|------------------------------------------------|----------------------------------------------------|------------|
| data    | [array](`array`) - [like](`like`)              | Volume data                                        | _required_ |
| space   | [NeuroSpace](`neuroim.neuro_space.NeuroSpace`) | Spatial metadata                                   | _required_ |
| label   | [str](`str`)                                   | Volume label                                       | `''`       |
| indices | [array](`array`) - [like](`like`)              | For sparse volumes, the indices of non-zero values | `None`     |

## Returns {.doc-section .doc-section-returns}

| Name   | Type                                     | Description                                       |
|--------|------------------------------------------|---------------------------------------------------|
|        | [NeuroVol](`neuroim.neuro_vol.NeuroVol`) | DenseNeuroVol, SparseNeuroVol, or LogicalNeuroVol |

## R Equivalent {.doc-section .doc-section-r-equivalent}

neuroim2::NeuroVol
